courses.title: Post-harvest Engineering of Cereals, Pulses and Oilseeds

lectures.t: General unit operations in agricultural process engineering and importance 1 of these unit operations in grain processing; Structure and composition of cereals, pulses and oil seeds

Cleaning and grading: Principles of cleaning, scalping, sorting and grading; 4 screens, different types of screen separators, fixed and variable aperture screens, capacity and effectiveness of screens, sieve analysis; various types of separators as specific gravity, magnetic, disc, spiral, pneumatic, inclined belt draper, velvet roll separator, colour sorter, cyclone separator

Drying: Moisture content and water activity, free moisture, bound moisture 6 and equilibrium moisture content, isotherm, hysteresis effect, EMC determination; Psychrometric chart and its use in drying; Drying principles and theory, thin layer and deep bed drying analysis, falling rate and constant rate drying periods, maximum and decreasing drying rate periods, drying equations, mass and energy balance, Shedd's equation; Drying methods (conduction, convection, radiation, batch, continuous); Different types of grain dryers (bin, flat bed, LSU, columnar, RPEC, fluidized, rotary and tray), tempering during drying; dryer performance
Principles of grain storage; different types of grain storage structures; deep 3 bin and shallow bin; design of a silo, structural and functional requirements of a grain storage go-down

Milling of oilseeds: preconditioning of oilseeds, mechanical expression, 3 screw press, hydraulic press, solvent extraction method, refining of oil, stabilization of rice bran

Separation processes: principle and equipment for sedimentation of 3 solids in liquid and solids in air; Principle and operation of tubular bowl centrifuge and disc bowl centrifuge

Preparatory weaving: Winding, Warping, Sizing, beaming, drawing and 2 denting; Weaving: Concept of weaving, Handloom, Primary and secondary motions of loom
Shedding, picking, Beatup, Loom timing, Take-up and Let-off motions
Basic woven fabric structure and design; Knitting: Concept of knitting
Basic Yarn testing: Count, Twist, tensile strength, CSP, Hairiness, Fabric 4 testing: GSM, EPI, PPI, Tensile strength, tearing strength, Bursting strength
Chemical composition of plant and animal fibers - Natural and added 2 impurities; Pre-treatment of natural fiber for surface cleaning/ removing impurities ­ Desizing

Scouring; Degumming; Bleaching- reductive, oxidative; combined scouring 3 and bleaching; Woollenization; Mercerization; fluorescent brightening agents; de-pigmentation; cottonisation; Carbonization, Felting of wool
Value-addition of natural fiber by colouration- Introduction to dyes and 2 pigments; Classification of dyes based on the source and application
The colouration of natural fibers- Direct, Acid, basic, Reactive, Vat
Dyeing machine; Dyeing of different textile forms-Hank yarn, Cone, Loose
Demand, supply and producer's surplus of agri commodities: nature and
Pricing and promotion strategies: pricing considerations and approaches ­ cost based and competition based pricing; market promotion ­ advertising
Facilitating functions ­ packaging, branding, grading, quality control and
Integration, efficiency, costs and price spread: Meaning, definition and types of market integration; marketing efficiency, marketing costs, margins and price spread
